title: CommitmentRecord Schema
description: A System Log record representing a deliberate commitment by a Domain Authority, Meta Authority, or the orchestrator acting under explicit authorization.

record_id.description: Unique identifier for this record (UUID v4).

prev_record_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the previous record's canonical JSON, or 'genesis' for the first record.

timestamp_utc.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when this record was created.

actor_id.description: Pseudonymous identifier of the actor making this commitment.

actor_role.description: Role of the actor at the time of commitment.

commitment_type.description: The type of commitment being made. Use 'system_physics_activation' when committing a new version of cfg/system-physics.json to the system log; use 'system_physics_rollback' when reverting to a prior version. Use 'novel_synthesis_verified' when a Domain Authority confirms a novel synthesis event is valid; use 'novel_synthesis_rejected' when the Domain Authority determines it is a false positive. Use 'cross_domain_synthesis_verified' when Domain Authorities from both domains in a cross-domain synthesis pair confirm the identified structural similarity is valid; use 'cross_domain_synthesis_rejected' when either Domain Authority determines the similarity is not meaningful. Cross-domain synthesis commitments require dual approval — both domain authorities in the pair must independently commit. Use 'domain_role_assignment' when a Domain Authority or authorized role assigns a domain-scoped role to a user within their domain; use 'domain_role_revocation' when a domain role is removed from a user.

subject_id.description: UUID or identifier of the subject (domain pack ID, session ID, student pseudonymous ID, etc.).

subject_version.description: Semantic version of the subject (if applicable).

subject_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the subject content at the time of commitment (e.g., domain pack JSON hash).

summary.description: Human-readable one-line description of what is being committed.

close_type.description: For session_close commitment types: how the session ended.

close_reason.description: For forced session closes: brief structured reason.

references.description: Optional list of related record IDs (e.g., the prior domain pack commitment being superseded).

metadata.description: Additional structured metadata. Must not contain PII or transcript content. When commitment_type is 'novel_synthesis_rejected', metadata MUST include a 'denial_rationale' object with two required sub-keys: 'reason_category' (enum: hallucination | out_of_domain | logical_error | insufficient_evidence | duplicate_of_known_pattern | other) and 'authority_note' (string, max 512 chars — the Domain Authority's written explanation of the denial). When commitment_type is 'novel_synthesis_verified', metadata SHOULD include 'domain_pack_id', 'model_id', and 'model_version' to enable corpus indexing for the SLM verified-synthesis replay path.
